recommendation: Codify the recognition/act split this de-barreling made concrete: when a fused @rubric dimension names two acts, judge each half separately as RECOGNIZED (a deterministic engine elsewhere in the same suite already asserts it as a boolean -> drop it from the rubric, it was never agentic) or genuinely AGENTIC (no engine boolean can assert it -> it earns its own rubric slot). manage-skill-dirs' `induces_and_previews_matched_skills` split exactly on this line: `induces` is pattern-recognition already covered by the two boolean 'Induce a pattern from a sample path' scenarios in the same suite, so fusing it into the rubric was redundant, not agentic; `previews_matched_skills` (surfacing the matched SKILL.md files to the user) has no engine assertion anywhere and stays rubric-graded alongside `confirms_before_writing_config`. Distinguish this from ssa-lowering (#308): there the fused halves had NO boolean backstop, so the fix closed an actual false-green path; here both halves were already independently asserted, so the fix is redundancy cleanup at unchanged max/threshold -- same mechanical move (split a dimension), two different severities of defect underneath it, and the severity call (false-green vs redundant) is what should gate priority, not the split itself. Also worth codifying: the per-surviving-dimension ablation table used here (a control profile, plus one holding-everything-but-X mutant per surviving dimension, each proven independently loseable) is a cheap repeatable check that a de-barreling didn't silently collapse the threshold. Retirement note: this CR left no plan.md/combat log at all -- it landed as a direct one-line commit narrowing an already-frozen scenario (self-cleared Clearance), so sdd:plan-retirement's distilled-gate is moot here (nothing to retire; `distills` above names the branch/PR identifier for provenance only, not an actual plan file that could be swept).
evidence: b74df3d3 (PR #373, manage-skill-dirs): 'The induces half is dropped from the rubric -- it is independently backstopped by the deterministic Induce a pattern from a sample path boolean scenarios in the same suite ... Redundancy cleanup, not a false-green fix: the fused act was already boolean-backstopped'

342fd770 (PR #372, spec-anchors -- the SDD-side sibling unit of the same #370 issue): identical split and identical ablation table (control PASS at 2, no-preview mutant FAIL at 1, no-confirm mutant FAIL at 1); already distilled SDD-side in .agents/specs/sdd/ledger/strategy.2d9bbc.jsonl seq 5 (distills: 371-recut-misalignment-given), which explicitly could not reach the ACED half since it is out of the SDD ledger's scope

issue #370 body: 'Unlike the ssa-lowering case, both are backstopped: each fused half is independently asserted by a plain boolean scenario in the same suite, so the fusion is redundant (weaker-but-not-false-green), not a false-green path -- lower priority accordingly'

git log --diff-filter=D over .agents/plans/*370* and *skill-dirs* returns nothing -- no plan/log file was ever created or deleted for this CR, confirming it landed outside the mission-loop plan machinery entirely

recommendation: The M2 CFG-re-derivation sweep caught a corpus-wide staleness no mechanical check would have found: 7 ACED skills plus one spec README still hardcoded the RETIRED suite-location convention (artifacts/specs/<feature-name>/, superseded by the colocated project-spec-node model), and it surfaced only because the owner personally caught it while ratifying one node (add-scenario) -- the node's own cold impl-judge pass had not covered the stale step either. Recommend a mechanical drift check for exactly this shape: whenever a design decision retires a path/directory/naming convention, register the retired term so a corpus-wide grep (skills + spec READMEs, not just the touched node) runs at verify time and flags any remaining mention -- the same spirit as the referenced-artifact-escalation guard already at the SDD spec gate (which catches broken artifact refs), generalized from broken links to retired-convention mentions. This is a different axis from the already-known 'coverage audit must scan all nodes' lesson (a scenario-coverage-completeness miss); this is a stale-terminology-across-the-corpus miss, and both point at the same root gap: no mechanical corpus-wide sweep runs unless a human happens to think to run one.
evidence: 304-m2-eval-suite-sweep.c38aa5.jsonl correction line: 'Owner-flagged during add-scenario ratification: the ACED skills hardcoded the RETIRED suite location artifacts/specs/<feature-name>/ ... Swept the location/binding wording across 7 skills + 1 spec-README ... grep artifacts/specs across plugins/aced/skills is now zero. Cold impl-judge re-verified add-scenario's two LOCATE scenarios PASS against the corrected step (the step my node-9 impl-judge originally didn't cover -- the gap the owner caught)'

recommendation: Recurring pattern, now 3 confirmed instances: a plan's own frontmatter `status` field is left stale (draft or in_progress, with pending-looking todos) even after every todo is checked off and the CR is fully merged to main. This does not block anything mechanically today -- plan-retirement gates on the caller-supplied source-closed judgment plus the ledger's own `distills` flag, never the plan's own `status` field -- but discover-plans surfaces resumable plans by file presence, and a stale `status: draft` on a 100%-complete, already-merged mission risks misreporting it as still open work to a future session. This is the same durability-discipline gap already codified for the combat log's `correction` line (write it at mission finalize even if nothing flushed it earlier); recommend the conductor apply the identical discipline to its own plan frontmatter -- write the terminal `status` (done) as an unconditional part of handoff, not left to be caught incidentally at a later retro.
evidence: .agents/plans/304-m2-eval-suite-sweep.plan.md frontmatter: status: draft, all 14 todos marked status: completed, yet PR #355 merged 2026-07-21T16:45:28Z
.agents/plans/304-m3-eval-artifact-migration.plan.md frontmatter: status: draft, all 5 todos marked completed/[DONE], yet PR #361 merged 2026-07-22T07:43:21Z

cross-ref, SDD ledger not ACED: .agents/specs/sdd/ledger/strategy.2d9bbc.jsonl seq 5 already flagged the identical shape once for 371-recut-misalignment-given.plan.md ('still on disk marked status: in_progress with most todos pending, despite the CR being fully merged (PR #374)') as an already-flagged unratified plan-bookkeeping-drift pattern -- this entry adds 2 further ACED-side occurrences confirming the pattern recurs across projects, not only within SDD's own corpus
